model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Michigan is the pick because its season-long predictive profile is stronger and more complete, with elite top-10 offense and top-two defense in the available ratings, while Iowa State enters this matchup still dealing with the loss of lead guard Keshon Gilbert for the tournament. The Cyclones absolutely have a credible path by turning this into a defensive, turnover-heavy grinder, but Michigan’s higher offensive ceiling and stronger overall efficiency margin make the Wolverines the safer Final Four choice.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced edge to Michigan over a shorthanded Iowa State
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan owns the stronger full-season two-way profile, and Iowa State’s missing lead guard lowers the Cyclones’ offensive margin for error in what is otherwise a dangerous defensive matchup.
evidence: Michigan is No. 2 in both KenPom and Torvik in the available ratings, with adjusted offense around 126.7-127.7 and adjusted defense around 88.4-90.5.
ESPN reporting indicates Iowa State guard Keshon Gilbert is out for the tournament, a meaningful loss for the Cyclones’ ballhandling and offensive creation.
Available injury reporting did not show a confirmed Michigan absence comparable to Iowa State’s Gilbert loss.
Iowa State still has a credible upset path through defensive pressure and a low-possession game, which keeps confidence moderate.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
